summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that YesWiki's raw HTML wiki markup feature permits stored XSS attacks. User content wrapped in ""..."" is returned without sanitization when allow_raw_html is enabled, and the injected JavaScript would execute in the browser context of any user viewing the page.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate successful injection of JavaScript code into a wiki page using the raw HTML syntax, confirming that the payload is stored and would execute when the page is viewed.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction bundle set up YesWiki with default configuration (allow_raw_html=true), created a page with XSS payload using the ""..."" syntax, and confirmed that the malicious script is present in the page source without sanitization.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks if the XSS payload string 'EV0HUNT_XSS_POC_SUCCESSFUL' or script tags are present in the page HTML, distinguishing successful injection from normal sanitized output.
evidence_produced_by_run: XSS payload successfully injected into page content via ""<script>..."" syntax
The malicious script appears in page source without HTML entity encoding
The allow_raw_html configuration enables this attack by default
Multiple XSS vectors (script, img onerror, svg onload) all succeed

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the YesWiki repository on the host, checks out the doryphore-dev branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
